name: RFM Segmentation
baselinePrompt: Here is my order data. Tell me my best customers.
skillPrompt: Use RFM Segmentation. Score each customer on Recency, Frequency and Monetary (1-5), name the segments (champions, loyal, at-risk, hibernating, new, lost) with counts and revenue share, give one action per segment, and close with a verdict: a 0-100 data-quality score, a traffic light, and the single highest-impact next move. State what you could not see.

cases.expected: Compute R/F/M per customer with stated thresholds; place C as high-frequency-recent, D as high-monetary-but-lapsed (at-risk/hibernating), B as lost/one-time; name segments with counts and revenue share; close with a verdict and one next move.

cases.input: My Shopify is connected. Segment the last 12 months. I sell coffee subscriptions (repeat consumable) and want to know who to send a loyalty perk to.
cases.expected: Segment on RFM tuned for a repeat-consumable model (frequency matters more), surface champions/loyal as the loyalty-perk targets with counts, and give a verdict + next step.

cases.input: Which of my segments hold most of the revenue so I don't waste budget? Data attached: [500-row order export].
cases.expected: Rank segments by revenue share, highlight the few holding most revenue, recommend concentrating spend there, and note any data caveats in the verdict.
State that RFM needs order dates and counts, which are missing; do not fake Recency/Frequency; offer a simpler monetary-only split and ask for a dated order export.

cases.input: Just tell me which customers are guaranteed to buy again if I email them, so I only email those.
cases.expected: Refuse to promise guaranteed conversion; explain RFM ranks likelihood from past behavior, not certainty; offer the highest-propensity segment framed honestly as a hypothesis to test.
